mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for corporate earnings, mechanically increasing present values and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger immediate equity risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns remain positive in > 55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.2pp, or the signal fails to separate bull/bear outcomes with directional accuracy below 45%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, which sustains corporate revenue growth expectations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 60-day forward returns in > 60% of triggered cases, or the interaction term loses predictive power when added to a baseline model with p-value > 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing capacity utilization, which translates to higher corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns during recovery phases, with out-of-sample R-squared below 0.01 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect economic expansion that supports consumer spending and equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following week.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero predictive power for 5-day forward returns with Rank IC consistently near zero across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation surpasses a 2.5% annual threshold, markets price in aggressive central bank tightening that compresses equity multiples, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 60-day returns in > 65% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 2.5% threshold, invalidating the inflation-risk-off channel.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real GDP and retail sales confirms synchronized macroeconomic expansion, which reduces recession probabilities and supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined GDP-retail signal yields negative 20-day returns in > 50% of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a late-cycle expansion signals capacity constraints and impending economic slowdown, triggering short-term equity sell-offs that produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following week.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines fail to predict negative 5-day returns during late-cycle phases, with directional accuracy below 45% in walk-forward validation.
